text.title: finops-agentic-workflow Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for finops-agentic-workflow as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agent identifies "low CPU" resources as idle and proposes rightsizing or termination.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A service may be I/O-bound, GPU-bound, or network-bound; CPU utilization alone is a dangerously incomplete signal. Rightsizing on CPU alone breaks workloads. Always include memory, network, GPU, and disk IOPS before classifying a resource as idle.

failure-mode.description: LLM proposes spot instances for stateful workloads ("save 70%!") without confirming checkpointing is implemented.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Spot interruption on a stateful workload without checkpointing produces a data-loss incident. Force the agent to check for checkpoint configuration before recommending spot, and mark the recommendation blocked until checkpointing is confirmed.

failure-mode.description: RI/SP recommendations from LLMs that assume usage continues linearly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs extrapolate from recent history without accounting for planned workload changes, seasonal patterns, or architecture migrations. Force the agent to state the assumption explicitly: "this assumes above-70% steady-state usage continues for 12 months." Human signs off on the assumption, not just the recommendation.

failure-mode.description: Tag-fix automations rewriting owner tags based on CloudTrail data.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The original creator may have left the company; stale CloudTrail data produces wrong ownership attribution. Cross-reference HRIS or Backstage service catalog before assigning owner tags retroactively. Manual confirmation for any unverifiable ownership.

failure-mode.description: Auto-shutdown of "idle" non-prod environments at a fixed UTC time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A 18:00 UTC shutdown hits an APAC team's morning standup. Add timezone awareness to shutdown schedules — either per-region cron offsets or a timezone metadata tag on the resource. Query team timezone from a service catalog, not hardcode UTC.

failure-mode.description: Anomaly detection agents claiming spikes on less than 60 days of history.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Monthly batch jobs, billing cycle artifacts, and seasonal patterns all look like anomalies to a detector trained on short history. Train anomaly detectors on at least 60 days (two full billing cycles) before using them to page on-call.
